what: Why not one object per file?

title: Performance notes
text: want parallelize search , can split file vector chunks run match_files() chunk mirai. idea: List filter files usual. Split file vector N roughly equal chunks. Start N mirai daemons. Call match_files() chunk parallel. Combine resulting seekr_match vectors. rough example: searching simple pattern across 48,000 R files (roughly 500 MB text) took 80 seconds sequentially 20 seconds 8 daemons one machine. Files likely cached OS point. single data point, benchmark. Actual results depend storage device, number cores, file sizes, pattern complexity, many matches need materialized. small projects, overhead starting workers combining results probably worth added code. sequential workflow simpler usually fast enough.
code: library(seekr) library(mirai)  files <-   list_files() |>   filter_files(extension = "R")  # Sequential version, useful as a reference x <- match_files(files, "foo", "bar") attr(x, "exclusions") <- NULL  N <- 8L  mirai::daemons(N) mirai::everywhere(library(seekr))  split_files <- unname(split(files, cut(seq_along(files), N)))  matches_list <- mirai::mirai_map(   split_files,   \(files) match_files(files, "foo", "bar", .progress = FALSE) )[]  mirai::daemons(0)  y <- Reduce(c, matches_list, init = new_seekr_match())  identical(x, y) #> TRUE

Note : vector matches returned seekr() also contains exclusions() attribute, matches found, vector returned seek()/seekr() also contains empty_stage() attribute helps explain workflow became empty.
code: files <- list_files() filtered <- filter_files(files, extension = "R") x <- match_files(filtered, "(\\w+)_(\\w+)(?= <- function)", "\\2_\\1")  y <- seekr("(\\w+)_(\\w+)(?= <- function)", "\\2_\\1")  identical(x, y) #> [1] TRUE

title: List, inspect, open, and delete backups — backups
text: list_backups() last_backup() return tibble one row per backed-file following columns: id: integer backup identifier, derived subdirectory name. rows belonging backup operation share id. created_at: date-time backup created. operation: either "replace" (backup created replace_files()) "restore" (backup created restore_files()). description: optional description provided backup time, NA. original: absolute path original file. backup: absolute path backup copy. original_exists: whether original file still exists disk. backup_exists: whether backup copy still exists disk. size: size backup copy, fs_bytes value.

title: Filter files to search — filter_files
text: filter_files() keeps files matching extension path_pattern, exceeding max_file_size. Finally, exclude functions applied remaining files, discarding common non-text irrelevant files default. second step seek() pipeline, applied list_files() match_files(). Exclusion filters applied order: extension: keeps files whose extension provided list. path_pattern: keeps files whose normalized path matches pattern. max_file_size: excludes files larger given size. exclude_functions: applies named function remaining files. Files passed subsequent filter already excluded previous one. Details excluded files stored result can retrieved exclusions().

title: Filter files to search — filter_files

title: Filter files to search — filter_files
text: path character vector file paths filter. ... dots future extensions must empty. extension Optional character vector file extensions keep. Either: NULL (default): filtering extension; extensions kept. character vector extensions keep, without leading dot (e.g. c("R", ".Rmd", "qmd")). Extensions normalized matching: leading dots stripped, matching case-insensitive, duplicates ignored. last component compound extensions used (e.g. "tar.gz" uses "gz"), warning. path_pattern Optional pattern applied filter normalized file paths (see as_seekr_path()). Either: NULL (default): filtering path. string, interpreted regular expression via stringr::regex(). stringr_pattern object stringr::regex() stringr::fixed(). max_file_size Maximum file size bytes. Files larger value excluded. Default Inf, meaning files excluded size. Zero negative values treated Inf. exclude Named list functions used exclude unwanted files filtering. Either: NULL, disable additional exclude functions. named list functions, taking character vector normalized file paths returning logical vector length, TRUE means file excluded. Defaults exclude_functions, excludes common non-text irrelevant files. .progress Whether display progress messages. Default TRUE interactive sessions FALSE otherwise (see rlang::is_interactive()). Can set globally options(seekr.progress = FALSE).

title: Filter files to search — filter_files
text: character vector normalized absolute paths passed filters. Paths use representation as_seekr_path(). attribute "exclusions" always attached result, containing data frame one row per input file one column per exclusion function, detailing files excluded . Retrieve exclusions().

title: seekr 0.2.0
text: complete redesign seekr. seekr now much ambitious package built around different model search--replace. Instead providing small set helpers finding text files, now introduces full workflow listing files, filtering files, finding matches, inspecting refining results, staging replacements, modifying selected files, creating backups, restoring . release introduces seekr_match vector, implemented vctrs record-style (rcrd) vector. element represents one independent match one file, fields store file path, match location, matched text, planned replacement, surrounding context, related metadata. release intentionally backward compatible seekr 0.1.3. scope package expanded substantially, keeping previous API required maintaining two separate paradigms side side. previous API therefore removed, existing code using earlier versions seekr need rewritten new workflow-oriented API.
